name: Scoozi's Flourless Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup orange-flavored liqueur (such as Grand Marnier)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Butter a 9-inch springform pan and dust the inside with flour, tapping out excess.
3. Gently melt chocolate and butter together. Allow to cool slightly.
4. In a large mixer bowl, beat egg yolks, 4 tablespoons of sugar, and liqueur until light, about 5 minutes. Add the chocolate mixture and mix thoroughly.
5. In a clean, grease-free mixer bowl, beat egg whites, cream of tartar, and salt until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 3 tablespoons of sugar, beating well after each addition, then continue beating until soft, glossy peaks form.
6. Thoroughly mix about one-fourth of the egg whites into the chocolate mixture, then gently fold in the remaining whites.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake, turning the pan halfway every 30 minutes, until a wooden pick inserted comes out with moist but not wet crumbs, approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes.
8. Remove the pan from the wire rack and run a small knife around the edge of the pan to loosen the cake. Allow the cake to cool completely in the pan. As it cools, the cake will shrink back from the pan and fall slightly. When fully cooled, remove the sides of the pan and invert the cake onto a serving plate.
9. Serve with dollops of whipped cream.
